episodes.title: The Church Girl Who Became a Manson Killer
episodes.description:
Susan Atkins was once a church-going teenager who sang in choirs and cared for her dying mother. Just a few years later, she would become one of the most infamous members of the Manson Family and play a role in the brutal Tate murders that shocked the world.

In this episode of Dark Finds Explained, we examine the life of Susan Atkins from her troubled adolescence and introduction to Charles Manson, to her involvement in the murders of Gary Hinman and Sharon Tate. We explore how Manson's influence, cult indoctrination, and the apocalyptic ideology of "Helter Skelter" transformed a vulnerable young woman into a participant in one of the most notorious murder sprees in American history.

We'll also examine the investigation that exposed the Manson Family, Atkins' shocking jailhouse confessions, the Tate-LaBianca trial, her decades behind bars, and where she ultimately ranks on the Dark Finds Depravity Index.

Was Susan Atkins a victim of cult manipulation, a willing participant in evil, or something in between? Watch to the end as we break down one of the most disturbing transformations in true crime history.
